title: salvia sphacelioides subsp. anaglypha
intro: Salvia sphacelioides subsp. anaglypha, also known as Anaglypha, is a species of flowering plant in the sage family, native to South Africa. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can be used to treat various health issues. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting immune function. The herbal preparations of Salvia sphacelioides subsp. anaglypha include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by infusing the leaves in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
